## Loop 226 - 2026-06-06

- Task: Move TTS enqueue helper into `xinyu_bridge_desktop_events.py`.
- Why: `_maybe_enqueue_tts` is the final side effect of the Desktop chat-finished event path. It gates TTS by finished/ok status, owner-private payload, active output, and non-empty reply, then enqueues a TTS expression. Keeping it with Desktop event adapters closes the chat-finished side-effect chain outside core runtime orchestration.

- Result: Added `xinyu_bridge_turn_live_state.py` and `xinyu_bridge_turn_transport_sidecars.py`, reducing `xinyu_bridge_turn_sidecars.py` from 584 lines to 478 without moving high-risk prompt skeleton text. Split slow-live reply adjustment into repair, shape, rendering, sticker, and pipeline service modules, reducing `xinyu_bridge_slow_live_reply_adjustments.py` from about 501 lines to 318. Split desktop proactive projection/publish from routes, reducing `xinyu_bridge_desktop_proactive_routes.py` to 447. Split action followup routes into `xinyu_bridge_action_followups.py`, reducing `xinyu_bridge_action_routes.py` to 291. Split Codex policy markers and owner-direct policy into service modules, reducing `xinyu_bridge_codex_policy.py` to 155. Split Codex report learning staging into `xinyu_bridge_learning_codex_reports.py`, reducing `xinyu_bridge_learning.py` to 293. Split renderer debug prompt dump into `xinyu_bridge_renderer_debug.py`, reducing `xinyu_bridge_renderer.py` to 259. Split proactive QQ outbox claim logic into `xinyu_bridge_proactive_outbox_claim.py`, reducing `xinyu_bridge_proactive_delivery_routes.py` to 225.
- Verification: Code-intel doctor passed with required tools present. Focused turn-sidecar tests passed with `19 passed`; slow-live turn passed repeatedly with `65 passed`; desktop proactive plus aliases passed with `40 passed`; merged touched-module regression passed with `113 passed`; Codex report material smoke passed; utility routes passed with `7 passed`; Codex report staging focused passed with `2 passed`; Codex policy focused passed with `15 passed`; renderer plus aliases passed with `19 passed`; proactive outbox/share/http focused passed with `13 passed`; expanded bridge regression passed with `423 passed`.
- Boundary checklist: Old public exports and runtime alias identities remain in the original facade modules. Monkeypatch-sensitive dependencies are injected from the old modules into the new services for slow-live reply repairs, desktop proactive projection/publish, action followups, Codex policy, proactive outbox claim, and renderer debug. New live-turn source modules were added to `BRIDGE_RUNTIME_SOURCE_RELS`.
- Risk: Medium. This was a broad multi-agent integration pass, but each write scope was isolated and the expanded bridge regression passed. The highest-risk prompt skeleton text and Codex runtime execution/finalization/dream/watchdog flows were intentionally not moved.
- Rollback: Move each service implementation back into its original facade module and delete the corresponding new module; old wrappers keep rollback mechanical. For live-turn sidecars, remove the new runtime source digest entries if the helper modules are deleted.
- Next: Continue with the remaining largest modules: `xinyu_bridge_slow_live_turn.py`, `xinyu_bridge_codex_runtime.py`, `xinyu_bridge_autonomous_maintenance.py`, `xinyu_bridge_desktop_snapshot.py`, `xinyu_bridge_turn_finish_sidecars.py`, and remaining desktop/private ecosystem route boundaries.

- Result: Fixed a malformed/garbled `CODEX_AMBIGUOUS_REQUEST_MESSAGE` in `xinyu_bridge_codex_runtime.py` while removing blank-line noise, matching the correct text already used by `xinyu_bridge_codex_execution.py`. Split session lifecycle/key/tail/cleanup into service modules. Split turn finish bindings, external plugin self-thought/native calls, private ecosystem grant/browser helpers, proactive context tail/thread/feedback, desktop snapshot projection, desktop self-action labels, desktop proactive route bindings/payload/ack, turn pipeline entry/decision/routes, turn sidecar prompt/state/owner/context/report helpers, autonomous thought sidecars, Codex finalization response/reports, action dispatch/finish/support/layer turn, learning ingest/study helpers, and renderer payload/context/trace. Updated runtime source digest coverage for newly split runtime-facing service modules and verified no missing digest paths.
- Verification: Codex runtime plus aliases passed with `92 passed`; session focused passed with `41 passed`; turn finish focused passed with `11 passed`; external plugin focused passed with `42 passed`; private ecosystem focused passed with `44 passed`; proactive context focused passed with `11 passed`; desktop snapshot focused passed with `38 passed`; desktop self-action focused passed with `29 passed`; desktop proactive/snapshot focused passed with `45 passed`; turn pipeline focused passed with `45 passed`; turn sidecars plus prompt focused passed with `23 passed` and `14 passed`; autonomous/proactive focused passed with `143 passed`; Codex finalization focused passed with `80 passed`; action focused passed with `34 passed`; learning focused passed with `9 passed`; renderer focused passed with `14 passed`; runtime/security/digest/health focused passed with `8 passed`; expanded bridge regression passed with `424 passed`; adjacent focused pack passed with `62 passed`; initiative/codex/renderer smoke checks passed.
- Boundary checklist: Old facade modules still expose public entry points and runtime alias identities. Monkeypatch-sensitive dependencies continue to be pulled from facade globals for session tail, turn finish bindings, turn prompt injection, desktop proactive routes, private ecosystem routes, action routes, learning helpers, renderer wrappers, and autonomous maintenance runners. A temporary overlap was avoided by closing the prompt-injection worker after `turn_sidecars` created that module. A desktop proactive stale reference surfaced during combined self-action tests and was resolved by accepting the worker's final facade/bindings integration; the combined desktop tests now pass.
- Risk: Medium. This was a very broad multi-agent pass with many new service modules. The main remaining architectural weight is intentional facade/compatibility code, especially `xinyu_bridge_slow_live_turn.py` and `xinyu_bridge_codex_runtime.py`; further reduction there needs careful handling because tests monkeypatch many facade globals.
- Rollback: Move each service implementation back into its original facade and delete the corresponding new modules. If rolling back turn/action/desktop/learning/renderer splits, remove the related `BRIDGE_RUNTIME_SOURCE_RELS` entries as well.
- Next: Continue service-readiness cleanup around the largest remaining compatibility facades: `xinyu_bridge_slow_live_turn.py`, `xinyu_bridge_codex_runtime.py`, `xinyu_bridge_desktop_snapshot.py`, `xinyu_bridge_autonomous_maintenance.py`, and route families still above ~250 lines.

- Rollback: Move helper implementations back into their original facade modules and delete new helper modules. For the promise marker repair, restore the previous marker table only after fixing string literal syntax; otherwise imports will fail.

- Risk: Medium. The Codex execution path now has one extra indirection, but the default backend is covered against the old foreground/background behavior and facade alias tests still pass.
- Rollback: Remove `xinyu_bridge_codex_execution_backend.py`, inline the old foreground/background branch in `runtime_codex_execute`, remove state persistence/proactive DTO contract additions and tests, and remove the new digest/manifest references.

Tests now bind those contracts to `xinyu_qq_outbox_client.py` and `xinyu_qq_outbox_dispatcher.py` behavior: claim payload shape, ack payload shape, private target validation, OneBot success/error/timeout mapping, proactive vs ordinary sent-delivery routes, disabled visible dispatch, sent ack config gates, and disabled-after-claim failed ack.

HTTP dispatch tests now lock GET/POST `/probe` routing and timeout behavior.

The blocker now reflects the remaining work after ownership moved: `/turn/current` operator DTOs still call `runtime.health_snapshot`, service health DTOs are not yet consumed across routes, and rollback smoke coverage is still missing.

## Loop 441 - 2026-06-09

- Risk: Low to medium. The dry-run worker response shape became stricter and more truthful for queued versus fallback-required states, but default runtime selection still uses the in-process backend.
- Rollback: Remove the new worker lifecycle methods/record type, restore dry-run execute to its prior response shape, remove the new worker-client tests, and drop the five satisfied worker gates from the enablement checklist.
- Next: Close the two remaining codex worker gates: `rollback_unsets_runtime_backend_attr_smoke` and `facade_route_payload_contract_unchanged_smoke`. Only then consider marking codex worker enablement ready.

- Rollback: Remove the two external_action smoke files from validation, restore the previous owner-private capability expectations, restore the old external_action blocker text, and keep the existing dry-run backend contract unchanged.
- Next: Add a real external_action route/facade backend-selection seam before reconsidering split readiness. `desktop_surface` remains S3 preflight-ready but blocked on event-stream/snapshot DTO process-split readiness.

## Loop 449 - 2026-06-09

- Task: Harden `proactive_delivery` terminal ack idempotency before service split.
- Why: QQ/proactive adapters can retry or deliver late failure acks. A future service boundary must not let duplicate `sent` or late `failed` mutate already-terminal delivery state.

  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_proactive_transport_health_contract.py`

  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q`
- Result: `ack_qq_outbox_message()` now treats `sent` and `dead` as terminal and returns an accepted no-op for duplicate/late acks. `acknowledge_proactive_qq_message()` now treats `sent` dispatch/request state as terminal and ignores duplicate `sent` or late `failed/queued/dry_run` writes. Route wrappers already publish delivery/outbound only when `ack_recorded=True`, so idempotent terminal acks do not double-publish.
- Readiness: `proactive_delivery` transport preflight remains ready, but process split stays blocked. The blocker is now narrowed to route backend selection and durable state-store ownership, not ack idempotency.
- Verification: Focused proactive/serviceization tests passed with `47 passed`; expanded regression passed with `214 passed`; runtime digest returned `missing_count 0`; default test suite passed with `1852 passed`.
- Boundary checklist: Public routes and payload aliases are unchanged. Claim-id mismatch and message-not-found remain accepted no-op rejections. No external QQ process or adapter was started.
- Risk: Low to medium. Late terminal acks now intentionally stop mutating queue/request/dispatch state; retryable `failed` before terminal status still follows the existing retry/dead path.
- Rollback: Remove the terminal-state no-op branches from `xinyu_qq_outbox.py` and `xinyu_proactive_presence.py`, remove `tests/test_proactive_delivery_idempotency.py`, and restore the previous proactive_delivery blocker text.
- Next: Add `proactive_delivery` route backend selection seam, then extract a local state-store adapter for request/dispatch/outbox ownership.

Wired route backend selection into `/proactive`, `/proactive/ack`, `/qq/outbox/claim`, `/qq/outbox/ack`, and `/desktop/proactive/ack`; QQ outbox fast paths are covered because HTTP dispatch prefers fast methods. Removing `_proactive_delivery_route_backend` restores the current in-process facades.

- Boundary checklist: Default behavior is unchanged because the backend is disabled unless explicitly attached to runtime. The dry-run backend does not mutate proactive/request/outbox state and does not start an external QQ process.
- Risk: Medium. This adds a new runtime interception point across five public routes, including fast methods. Tests cover default no-op, enabled dry-run, desktop ack, and attr rollback.
- Rollback: Remove `xinyu_bridge_proactive_delivery_route_backend.py`, remove backend helper calls from proactive/desktop route modules, remove new tests/smoke/contract entries, and restore the previous blocker text.
- Next: Extract a local `proactive_delivery` state-store adapter so request state, dispatch state, and QQ outbox queue ownership are explicit behind one boundary.

- Rollback: Remove `xinyu_bridge_proactive_delivery_state_store.py`, restore path helpers in `xinyu_qq_outbox.py` and `xinyu_proactive_presence.py`, remove state-store tests/manifest entries, and set `proactive_delivery.process_split_ready` back to false.

- Result: Added `InProcessDesktopSelfActionApprovalBackend`, approval command/readiness DTOs, runtime backend attr selection, and in-process helper functions for pending queue lookup, patch executor attachment, and approval replies. `/desktop/self-action/approval` now parses payload then delegates execution to the approval backend. `dispatch_desktop_self_action_approval_result()` no longer carries `runtime`; runtime-specific dependencies are held at the backend fallback edge. Existing private runtime alias functions remain present as wrappers.
- Readiness: `self_action_approval_backend_contract` is now a satisfied `desktop_surface` gate. The `desktop_surface` split blocker is narrowed to snapshot state-store ownership.
- Verification: Focused approval/desktop/serviceization tests passed with `80 passed`; expanded serviceization/self-action regression passed with `265 passed`; runtime digest returned `missing_count 0`; default test suite passed with `1909 passed`.
- Boundary checklist: Public route, payload aliases, QQ approval commands, `authorizeCodex`, `authorizeExisting`, runtime facade alias identity, and in-process rollback behavior remain unchanged. No external desktop or Codex process was started by the seam.
- Risk: Medium. Approval execution now has a backend interception point and dispatch helper signature changed internally. Tests cover route backend override, default helper behavior, alias identity, approval controls, and gateway/patch executor regressions.
- Rollback: Remove `xinyu_bridge_desktop_self_action_approval_backend.py`, restore direct approval flow in `xinyu_bridge_desktop_self_action_routes.py`, restore the previous dispatch runtime parameter, remove approval backend contract/test entries, and restore the prior `desktop_surface` blocker text.
- Next: Move remaining snapshot-owned runtime state behind a local desktop surface state-store. Start with recent chat/memory buffers, then proactive inbox/history and snapshot deps before marking `desktop_surface` controlled split-ready.

- Boundary checklist: Public desktop routes and payload shapes are unchanged. In-process backend/fallback adapters still provide rollback, and no external desktop service process was started. Main chat/persona/semantic-fast paths remain protected and local.
- Risk: Medium. This flips a major boundary to controlled-ready. The readiness is contract/in-process split readiness, not an instruction to launch an external process automatically.
- Rollback: Set `desktop_surface.process_split_ready=False`, restore the previous process split gate text, restore growth-candidate root access and inbox runtime alias calls, and remove the updated readiness assertions.
- Next: Continue serviceization on the remaining not-ready boundaries: `learning_ingest`, `life_metabolism`, and `state_persistence`.

## Loop 577 - 2026-06-11

- Task: Add focused self-choice store coverage, fix its load/recover file-lock flush path, and register it as local-only state persistence.
- Why: `xinyu_self_choice_store.py` was the last unregistered local store, but it sits near `life_metabolism` and reply-policy side reads. It needed focused unit coverage before contract registration. The new tests exposed a real nested file-lock timeout in `load_or_recover()`: first-load and corrupt-recovery paths held the file lock while calling `_flush_locked()`, causing a 3-second timeout and recovery fallback.
- Files changed:
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_self_choice_store.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_self_choice_store.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_serviceization_contracts.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_serviceization_contracts.py`
  - `worklog/24h-refactor-progress.md`
- Commands:
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q tests\test_self_choice_store.py --durations=10`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m compileall -q xinyu_self_choice_store.py tests\test_self_choice_store.py`
  - `git diff --check -- xinyu_self_choice_store.py tests/test_self_choice_store.py`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q tests\test_self_choice_store.py tests\test_state_persistence_contract.py tests\test_serviceization_contracts.py`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe tests\smoke\life\xinyu_self_choice_store_smoke.py`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m compileall -q xinyu_self_choice_store.py tests\test_self_choice_store.py tests\test_serviceization_contracts.py xinyu_serviceization_contracts.py`
  - `git diff --check -- xinyu_self_choice_store.py tests/test_self_choice_store.py tests/test_serviceization_contracts.py xinyu_serviceization_contracts.py`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -c "from pathlib import Path; from xinyu_runtime_security import runtime_source_paths; missing=[str(p) for p in runtime_source_paths(Path('.')) if not p.exists()]; print('missing_count', len(missing)); [print(p) for p in missing]"`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe tests\smoke\runtime\integration\runtime_readiness_smoke.py --offline`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe tests\smoke\codex\codex_delegate_smoke.py`
- Result: `load_or_recover()` now releases the file lock before flushing the loaded/recovered state, preserving atomic writes while removing the 3-second nested-lock timeout. New focused tests cover default load, flush/reload, corrupt recovery backup+ledger, public snapshot privacy, known/unknown event impulses, and one-shot hibernation residue consumption. `state_persistence` now lists `xinyu_self_choice_store.py` and `tests/test_self_choice_store.py`.
- Readiness: Local persistence consolidation only. `life_metabolism` remains `process_split_candidate=False` and `process_split_ready=False`; self-choice policy, reply-path reads, metabolism event impulses, public route behavior, runtime facade, state schema, and split-ready service set were not changed.
- Verification: Self-choice focused tests dropped from roughly 18.3s to 0.22s and passed with `6 passed`; self-choice plus state-persistence/serviceization contract tests passed with `21 passed`; self-choice life smoke passed; compileall passed; `git diff --check` passed; runtime source check returned `missing_count 0`; default test suite passed with `2255 passed`. `codex_delegate_smoke.py` still fails on the pre-existing `xinyu_bridge_http_dispatch.py` marker gap for `"/codex/execute"` and `runtime.codex_execute`. Offline runtime readiness still fails only the pre-existing `mojibake_guard`.
- Boundary checklist: `xinyu_self_choice_store.py` is registered only under local-only `state_persistence`; it is explicitly guarded from split-ready contracts; `state_persistence` remains `process_split_candidate=False` and `process_split_ready=False`; split-ready services remain exactly `proactive_delivery`, `desktop_surface`, `desktop_event_stream`, `codex_execution`, `external_action`, and `health_diagnostics`.
- Risk: Medium-low. The production code change is small and fixes a real lock timeout, but the module is life/metabolism-adjacent. Focused tests, existing life smoke, and full suite passed.
- Rollback: Move the load-time flush back into the file-lock block, remove `tests/test_self_choice_store.py`, remove the self-choice contract guard, and remove the self-choice store/test from the `state_persistence` contract.
- Next: Continue with report/diagnostic ownership registration outside main chat/persona/semantic-fast hot paths.

- Result:
  - `codex_execution` is now built from `XINYU_CODEX_EXECUTION_*` env through `RuntimeServiceBindings`, no longer overwritten by mutable-state reset; lifecycle start injects the worker-client backend when configured and shutdown clears it back to in-process fallback.
  - `external_action` now has a runtime service handle with default disabled behavior and optional env-driven dry-run backend injection via `XINYU_EXTERNAL_ACTION_BACKEND=dry_run` or `XINYU_EXTERNAL_ACTION_DRY_RUN_ENABLED=true`; default routes still use current in-process facades.
  - `health_diagnostics` is now bound as `runtime._health_diagnostics_service`, started/stopped by runtime lifecycle, and `/health` prefers the runtime handle while preserving the old in-process builder fallback.
  - `codex_delegate_smoke.py` marker gap is fixed by adding explicit `/codex/execute` and `runtime.codex_execute` dispatch markers without changing table-driven route behavior.

## Loop 588 - 2026-06-11

## Loop 601 - 2026-06-12

- Task: Slim runtime service lifecycle start/stop wiring into explicit order contracts.
- Why: `start_background_tasks` and `shutdown` had repeated hand-written service handle start/stop blocks, making service boundary ordering harder to audit as more handles were added.
- Files changed:
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_bridge_runtime_lifecycle.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_bridge_runtime_lifecycle.py`
  - `worklog/24h-refactor-progress.md`
- Result:
  - Added `RUNTIME_SERVICE_STARTERS` and `RUNTIME_SERVICE_STOPPERS` as explicit lifecycle order contracts.
  - `start_background_tasks` now iterates the starter order instead of repeating every service start call.
  - `shutdown` now iterates the stopper order through a shared warning wrapper while preserving existing warning text and stop order.
  - Kept state persistence first in startup and last in shutdown.
  - Stored lifecycle function names instead of function objects, so monkeypatch/runtime extension points remain live.
  - Did not add service IDs, change readiness semantics, change background task behavior, or change service split readiness.

## Loop 607 - 2026-06-12

- Task: Pin the desktop event stream lifecycle boundary as app-owned, not runtime-service-owned.
- Why: `desktop_event_stream` is split-ready but its websocket lifecycle is owned by `run_bridge_app()` and `DesktopService`, while runtime lifecycle starters/stoppers own in-process bridge service handles. This exception needs an explicit guard before externalizing service adapters.

  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_desktop_service.py`

  - Added `DESKTOP_EVENT_STREAM_LIFECYCLE_BOUNDARY` to document that websocket start/stop is app-owned and not a runtime service starter.
  - Updated health provider and tests to use the centralized attr instead of repeating `"_desktop_event_stream_service"`.
  - Added a runtime lifecycle guard that `desktop_event_stream` is not added to `RuntimeServiceBindings`, `RUNTIME_SERVICE_STARTERS`, or `RUNTIME_SERVICE_STOPPERS`.

- Why: `diagnostic_reports` and `memory_governance_reports` are fixed local/not-ready services. Their readiness built module/test inventory inline on every call; serviceization prep benefits from a smaller, explicit per-handle inventory path while keeping missing-file semantics testable.
- Files changed:
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_bridge_local_report_services.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_local_report_services.py`
  - `worklog/24h-refactor-progress.md`
- Result:
  - Added `LocalReportServiceInventory` as a pure inventory snapshot DTO.
  - Moved contract module/test missing-path checks into `_contract_inventory`.
  - Cached inventory only after the handle is started; pre-start probes still re-read paths so existing dynamic/missing-file semantics are preserved.
  - Added a regression test proving pre-start readiness does not cache inventory before files appear.
  - Did not change report generation, HTTP routes, token requirements, service IDs, split readiness sets, or QQ gateway files.
- Commands:
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q tests\test_local_report_services.py tests\test_bridge_health_snapshot.py tests\test_serviceization_contracts.py tests\test_serviceization_readiness.py`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m compileall -q xinyu_bridge_local_report_services.py tests\test_local_report_services.py tests\test_bridge_health_snapshot.py`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q tests\test_state_persistence_service.py tests\test_chat_turn_service.py tests\test_learning_ingest_service.py tests\test_life_metabolism_service.py tests\test_local_report_services.py tests\test_codex_execution_service.py tests\test_external_action_service.py tests\test_desktop_surface_service.py tests\test_proactive_delivery_service.py tests\test_bridge_runtime_state.py tests\test_bridge_runtime_lifecycle.py tests\test_bridge_health_snapshot.py tests\test_serviceization_contracts.py tests\test_serviceization_readiness.py tests\test_codex_execution_contract.py tests\test_external_action_contract.py tests\test_desktop_surface_contract.py tests\test_desktop_event_stream_contract.py tests\test_health_diagnostics_preflight_contract.py tests\test_proactive_delivery_contract.py tests\test_bridge_app.py tests\test_codex_execution_backend.py tests\test_external_action_backend.py tests\test_desktop_surface_route_backend.py tests\test_proactive_delivery_route_backend.py tests\test_codex_execution_worker_client.py`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -c "from pathlib import Path; from xinyu_runtime_security import runtime_source_paths; missing=[str(p) for p in runtime_source_paths(Path('.')) if not p.exists()]; print('missing_count', len(missing)); [print(p) for p in missing]"`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -c "from xinyu_serviceization_contracts import process_split_ready_contracts, process_split_candidates, service_boundary_contracts; print('service_count', len(service_boundary_contracts())); print('split_candidates', sorted(c.service_id for c in process_split_candidates())); print('split_ready', sorted(c.service_id for c in process_split_ready_contracts())); print('local_not_ready', sorted(c.service_id for c in service_boundary_contracts() if not c.process_split_ready))"`
  - `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -c "from xinyu_bridge_http_dispatch_table import GET_ROUTE_DISPATCH, POST_ROUTE_DISPATCH; from xinyu_serviceization_contracts import service_boundary_contracts; known=set(GET_ROUTE_DISPATCH)|set(POST_ROUTE_DISPATCH); covered={r for c in service_boundary_contracts() for r in c.api_routes}; print('dispatch_known',len(known)); print('contract_routes',len(covered)); print('missing_count',len(known-covered)); print('extra',sorted(covered-known))"`
  - `git diff --check -- xinyu_bridge_local_report_services.py tests/test_local_report_services.py ..\..\..\..\worklog\24h-refactor-progress.md`
- Verification: Focused local-report/health/serviceization suite passed with `74 passed`. Combined service-handle/contract/app/backend/worker regression passed with `234 passed`. Compileall passed. Runtime source list check reported `missing_count 0`. Dispatch coverage reported `missing_count 0` with only the existing `/health` and `/life/metabolism/tickets` extras. Service-set audit stayed at 12 contracts with the same six split-ready and six local/not-ready boundaries. Scoped diff check passed.
- Boundary checklist: `diagnostic_reports` and `memory_governance_reports` remain local/not-ready. No service was promoted to split-ready. No QQ gateway files were changed.
- Risk: Low. Inventory construction was narrowed and tested; no route or report behavior changed.
- Rollback: Inline `_missing_paths` calls in `LocalReportServiceHandle.readiness` and remove `LocalReportServiceInventory` plus the new pre-start cache regression test.
- Next: Add hard tests for runtime source path completeness, fixed local/not-ready set, and state persistence isolation from split-ready modules.

## Loop 615 - 2026-06-12

- Task: Add hard serviceization guardrails for digest completeness, local-only services, and state persistence isolation.
- Why: Continuous serviceization needs tests that fail immediately if a runtime digest path goes missing, if a local/not-ready service is accidentally promoted, or if state persistence modules leak into split-ready contracts.

  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_serviceization_contracts.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_serviceization_readiness.py`
  - `worklog/24h-refactor-progress.md`
- Result:
  - Strengthened runtime digest coverage test to assert `runtime_source_paths(ROOT)` has no missing paths.
  - Added a fixed local/not-ready service-set assertion for `chat_turn`, `learning_ingest`, `life_metabolism`, `diagnostic_reports`, `memory_governance_reports`, and `state_persistence`.
  - Added readiness-level assertions that every local/not-ready service remains `candidate=False`, `ready=False`, and blocked by `not_process_split_candidate`.
  - Added a generic state persistence isolation test proving all `state_persistence.contract_modules` have zero overlap with split-ready contract modules.
  - Did not change runtime code, route behavior, service IDs, split readiness sets, or QQ gateway files.

## Loop 625 - 2026-06-12

- Task: Add an optional HTTP worker client scaffold for `codex_execution`.
- Why: `codex_execution` is the preferred serviceization pilot. The existing worker seam was dry-run only; a real external-process adapter scaffold is needed while keeping default behavior in-process and safe.
- Files changed:
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_bridge_codex_execution_worker_client.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_bridge_codex_execution_service.py`

  - Added `HttpCodexExecutionWorkerClient` with JSON transport injection, `/codex/execute` submit, `/codex/cancel` cancel, and response normalization to the existing worker response shape.
  - Added `build_codex_execution_worker_client`: no endpoint keeps the dry-run client; configured endpoint selects the HTTP client.
  - Updated `CodexExecutionServiceHandle` to pass endpoint and timeout config into the worker client factory.
  - Added tests for HTTP client factory selection, fake-transport execute/cancel, and service endpoint selection.
  - Default service startup remains in-process and does not inject a backend unless worker mode, endpoint, enablement, and health are configured.
  - Did not change `/codex/execute` payload shape, route behavior, service IDs, split readiness sets, or QQ gateway files.

## Loop 633 - 2026-06-12

- Task: Add a dry-run `codex_execution` worker service harness for the first process pilot shape.
- Why: `codex_execution` had an HTTP worker client scaffold, but no local process-compatible worker service harness implementing the same `/codex/execute` and `/codex/cancel` protocol. A dry-run harness lets the existing HTTP client be smoke-tested against a service boundary without executing real Codex work.
- Files changed:
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_bridge_codex_execution_worker_service.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_serviceization_contracts.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_runtime_security.py`

  - Added `CodexExecutionWorkerService` dry-run harness with `/health`, `/codex/execute`, `/codex/cancel`, and `/codex/completions` request handling.
  - Added `codex_execution_worker_service_transport` so `HttpCodexExecutionWorkerClient` can be tested against the service harness without starting a network server.
  - Added readiness metadata proving the worker harness is dry-run and does not execute runtime Codex.
  - Added tests for health, submit, cancel, completion outbox, not-ready fallback, invalid route handling, payload normalization, and HTTP client transport integration.
  - Added a standalone smoke script and declared the new worker service module/test/smoke in the `codex_execution` service contract.
  - Added the worker service source to `BRIDGE_RUNTIME_SOURCE_RELS` so runtime digest coverage remains complete.
  - Did not change `/codex/execute` route payloads, the runtime facade, default in-process behavior, worker env defaults, chat/persona/semantic-fast, or QQ gateway files.

## Loop 637 - 2026-06-12

- Task: Add split-ready process-harness guard coverage and a dry-run `health_diagnostics` provider registry service harness.
- Why: The first wave now had dry-run worker/service harnesses for `codex_execution`, `external_action`, `proactive_delivery`, and `desktop_surface`, but the guarantee was scattered across per-service tests. `health_diagnostics` also had an HTTP provider-registry client but no dry-run provider service endpoint for `/health/services/{service_id}`.
- Files changed:
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_bridge_health_provider_registry_service.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_serviceization_contracts.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu_runtime_security.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/xinyu.local.env.example`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_health_diagnostics_provider_registry_service.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_health_diagnostics_preflight_contract.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/test_serviceization_contracts.py`
  - `XinYu-Core/examples/agent-apps/xinyu/tests/smoke/bridge/health_diagnostics_provider_registry_service_smoke.py`
  - `docs/plans/XINYU-SERVICEIZATION-PLAN-2026-06-08.md`
  - `worklog/24h-refactor-progress.md`
- Result:
  - Added `HealthDiagnosticsProviderRegistryService`, a dry-run provider registry service harness with `/health` and `/health/services/{service_id}` handling.
  - Added `health_provider_registry_service_transport` so `HttpHealthDiagnosticsProviderRegistry` can call the harness through the same transport seam used by external provider registry pilots.
  - Added readiness metadata proving the harness is dry-run, does not mutate state, uses only existing service IDs, and rolls back to the in-process provider registry.
  - Added focused tests for readiness, `/health`, per-service health payloads, transport integration, fallback-required behavior, unknown route handling, and contract declaration.
  - Added smoke coverage through `health_snapshot -> provider registry -> HTTP registry client -> dry-run provider registry service transport`.
  - Added central serviceization guards proving all non-WS split-ready process harnesses are declared and dry-run safe, while `health_diagnostics` remains `provider_registry` and `desktop_event_stream` remains `ws_contract_only`.
  - Added `runtime_source_paths` explicit `missing_count == 0` guard and included the provider registry/service sources in `BRIDGE_RUNTIME_SOURCE_RELS`.
  - Documented that health provider registry pilots use runtime injection and do not replace the bridge `/health` route.
  - Did not change service IDs, HTTP bridge routes, runtime facade names, split/local sets, QQ gateway, `/chat`, persona, semantic-fast, or desktop websocket lifecycle.
